Q (expected_behavior=answer) asks about the agent-cli-scaler project's tools and why writeFile is separate from executeCommand. The corpus (corpus/agent-cli-scaler.README.md, lines 75-84) fully answers this: tools are executeCommand, writeFile, readFile, fetchScalerSite; writeFile is separate to avoid embedding multi-line HTML in a shell heredoc which mangles quoting. The answer falsely claimed 'I don't have that information in Rajveer's materials' (the info is present), missing every must_include fact (executeCommand, writeFile, readFile, fetchScalerSite, heredoc). It then conflated the project with the persona system's own plumbing, listing lookup_facts/search_knowledge/get_available_slots/book_meeting as 'the functions the agent currently provides.' Only lookup_facts and search_knowledge have any corpus support (a comment in persona.yaml lines 2-4); get_available_slots and book_meeting appear nowhere in the corpus, yet are asserted as true capabilities. grounded_ok=false (failed to answer with available corpus facts and substituted ungrounded specifics). hallucinated=true for the unsupported booking tools and for misrepresenting an unrelated tool list as responsive. refused_ok=N/A since expected_behavior=answer.

expected_behavior=answer, grounded_source=github:META_H. Verified every claim against /Users/rajveerbishnoi/AI_person/backend/app/data/corpus/META_H.README.md. No unsupported specifics, no hallucinations; faithfulness 1.0. refused_ok N/A (=true) since this is an answer item.
